142	          [Attributed(clauses=(Clause('c:leak', 'employment fell by 0.2 percentage points per '
143	                                                'robot'),))], B)),
144	      'a fabricated 0.2 passed because the source happened to say 10.25')
145	
146	# CROSS-SOURCE SYNTHESIS — the joint-heaviest criterion on the board (w=0.0800). The old gate deleted
147	# EVERY one of these, which is why our synthesis section was 210 words out of 8,012.
148	synth = [Attributed(clauses=(
149	    Clause('c:autor', 'computer capital substitutes for workers in a limited and well-defined set of '
150	                      'routine tasks'),
151	    Clause('c:bres', 'computer automation of such work has been correspondingly limited in its scope')),
152	    connective='while')]
153	check('CROSS-SOURCE SYNTHESIS survives the gate (it is the heaviest criterion, w=0.0800)',
154	      not A.validate_report(synth, B),
155	      'the comparison was DELETED — critical synthesis cannot reach the judge')
156	
157	check('a fabricated binding is REJECTED even when hidden inside a comparison',
158	      bool(A.validate_report([Attributed(clauses=(
159	          Clause('c:autor', 'computer capital substitutes for workers in routine tasks'),
160	          Clause('c:bres', 'task displacement drives the labor share down')), connective='while')], B)),
161	      'the multi-source lane let a fabricated binding through')
162	
163	# THE EVIDENCE-LAUNDERING ATTACK. The gate used to validate against `span + claim`, and `claim` is
164	# WRITTEN BY THE MODEL. So: model writes claim -> writer sees only claim -> gate checks writing against
165	# claim. THE GATE VALIDATED THE MODEL AGAINST ITSELF.
166	laundered = dict(CARDS[0], claim='Computerisation reduced employment by 47 percent across affected firms.')
167	check('EVIDENCE LAUNDERING: a figure the extractor invented in `claim` is REJECTED',
168	      bool(A.validate_report(
169	          [Attributed(clauses=(Clause('c:bres', 'computerisation reduced employment by 47 percent across '
170	                                                'affected firms'),))],
171	          CardBundle([laundered], g, P.JOURNAL_ONLY))),
172	      'THE MODEL VALIDATED ITSELF — a fabricated number shipped under a real citation')
173	
174	check('the gate NEVER validates against the model-authored `claim`',
175	      not re.search(r"(?m)^\s*src\s*=\s*f?['\"]?\{?span\}?\s*\{?claim\}?", asrc)
176	      and 'claim' not in inspect.signature(A.entailed_by_span).parameters,
177	      'the evidence-laundering path is open again: the gate validates the model against itself')
178	
179	# =================================================================================================
180	# 12-14. THE NEW LAW — the lanes that did not exist when this canary was written.
181	# =================================================================================================
182	# THE P0. Working-paper bytes wearing a journal's name. This is the card that scored us 0.4603.
183	r = B.resolve('c:ar')
184	check('THE P0: working-paper bytes labelled "Journal of Political Economy" CANNOT BE CITED',
185	      not r.ok and 'SOURCE_POLICY' in (r.refusal or ''),
186	      f'IT RESOLVED, and would have printed: {r.attribution!r}')
187	
188	check('an UNBOUND card (a DOI and a span, no manifestation) is REFUSED',
189	      not CardBundle([{'id': 'x', 'span': 'anything', 'claim': 'anything', 'authors': ['Z'],
190	                       'doi': '10.1/x', 'venue': 'Nature'}], g, P.JOURNAL_ONLY).resolve('x').ok,
191	      'a card with no bytes behind it was admitted — a DOI names a work, and a work has no bytes')
192	
193	check('the model may NOT name a source in its prose (voice is never inferred from surnames)',
194	      bool(A.validate_report(
195	          [Attributed(clauses=(Clause('c:autor', 'Bresnahan and colleagues show that computer capital '
196	                                                 'substitutes for workers in routine tasks'),))], B)),
197	      'the model typed a citation, and the gate accepted its choice of source')
198	
199	check('an OWNED sentence may not carry a number, and may not name a source',
200	      bool(A.validate_report([Owned(text='the effect is about 5 percentage points')], B))
201	      and bool(A.validate_report([Owned(text='Acemoglu is right about this')], B)))
202	
203	# =================================================================================================
204	# 15-17. THE ARTIFACT. The only question that cannot be fooled: WHAT IS IN THE FILE THE JUDGE READS?
205	# =================================================================================================
206	check('the judged release directory is SEALED — the composer CANNOT write the artifact',
207	      publisher.is_sealed(),
208	      'the release directory is writable: the composer can publish around the publisher')
209	
210	if RELEASE.exists():
211	    art = RELEASE.read_text()
212	    side = publisher.RELEASE / 'report.bindings.json'
213	    check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: no "the The <Journal>" doubling',
214	          not re.findall(r'\bthe The\b', art))
215	    check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: no [n] citation markers (the cleaner deletes them anyway)',
216	          not re.search(r'\[\d+\]', art))
217	    check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: no paragraph the gate emptied to a bare label',
218	          not [p for p in art.split('\n\n')
219	               if re.match(r'^\*{0,2}\[[A-Za-z /-]+\]\*{0,2}[.:]?$', p.strip())])
220	    check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: it has a sentence-hash-to-binding sidecar', side.exists())
221	    if side.exists():
222	        known = {e['sentence_hash'] for e in json.loads(side.read_text())['sentences']}
223	        orphan = []
224	        for line in art.splitlines():
225	            t = line.strip()
226	            if not t or t.startswith('#') or t.startswith('|') or t.startswith('**Table'):
227	                continue
228	            for s in publisher._sentences(t):
229	                if A.sentence_hash(s) not in known:
230	                    orphan.append(s)
231	        check('SHIPPED ARTIFACT: EVERY sentence in it resolves to a binding',
232	              not orphan, f'{len(orphan)} unbound sentence(s), e.g. {orphan[:1]}')
233	else:
234	    print('  [skip] no release on disk yet (run the composer with --write)')
235	
236	print()
237	if fails:
238	    print(f'** {len(fails)} FAILURE(S). THE DOOR IS OPEN. NOTHING SHIPS. **')
239	    for f in fails:
240	        print(f'    - {f}')
241	    raise SystemExit(1)
242	print('** GATE IS WIRED AND ON THE CRITICAL PATH. **')

content.text: The existing `test_release_boundary.py` is exactly the self-certification the task warns against: it `import`s `publisher` and calls `publish()` in-process with hand-built nodes. It never launches the real submission command. That is the lane the task says cannot be trusted. Let me understand the data model and the LLM seam so I can drive the real subprocess.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""A SYNTHETIC GRAPH WITH REAL BYTES. Every test fixture in this repo is now a BOUND card.
3	
4	The old canary handed the composer bare dicts — `{'authors': [...], 'span': '...', 'claim': '...'}` —
5	with no manifestation, no content hash and no offsets, and asserted things about them. That is a fixture
6	for a lane the fabrication no longer uses. Under the law a card like that DOES NOT EXIST, so a test built
7	on one proves nothing about the pipeline that ships.
8	
9	These fixtures bind. `bind_span()` slices real bytes, `verify_span()` re-checks them, and
10	`resolve_attribution()` decides what may be named — exactly as in production.
11	
12	THE THIRD FIXTURE IS THE P0 ITSELF: metadata that says `Journal of Political Economy`, bytes that are
13	the NBER working paper. Every suite gets to try to cite it, and every suite must fail.
14	"""
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import hashlib
18	import sys
19	from pathlib import Path
20	
21	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
22	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
23	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
24	
25	import provenance as P  # noqa: E402
26	
27	BRES_SPAN = 'Computer automation of such work has been correspondingly limited in its scope.'
28	AUTOR_SPAN = ('we contend that computer capital substitutes for workers in carrying out a limited and '
29	              'well-defined set of cognitive and manual activities, namely routine tasks')
30	AR_SPAN = 'we find that employment falls by 0.2 percentage points per robot per thousand workers'
31	LEAK_SPAN = 'productivity growth of 10.25 percent was observed across the sampled establishments'
32	
33	#: Long enough to clear the scholarly stub floor (1,200 words). Completeness is a property of the KIND:
34	#: a `journal_article` of 40 words is a stub, and the registry is right to say so.
35	_FILLER = ('The paper proceeds as follows. We describe the data, the identification strategy and the '
36	           'estimation. ') * 120
37	
38	
39	def build() -> tuple[P.Graph, list[dict]]:
40	    g = P.Graph()
41	
42	    def work(wid, authors, year, venue, doi, title='A study'):
43	        g.works[wid] = P.Work(id=wid, title=title, authors=authors, year=year, venue=venue,
44	                              doi=doi, kind='study')
45	
46	    def expr(eid, wid, kind):
47	        g.expressions[eid] = P.Expression(id=eid, work_id=wid, kind=kind, kind_basis='test fixture',
48	                                          attribution=P._attribution_for(kind, g.works[wid]))
49	
50	    def manif(mid, eid, wid, span, kind):
51	        text = _FILLER + span + ' ' + _FILLER
52	        g.manifestations[mid] = P.Manifestation(
53	            id=mid, expression_id=eid, work_id=wid, text=text,
54	            content_hash=hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest(), n_words=len(text.split()),
55	            locator='http://example/x', locator_status='RECORDED', fetched_by='test',
56	            text_field='fulltext',
57	            profile=dict(artifact_kind=kind, complete=True,
58	                         extractability=P.extractability(text), incomplete_because=[]))
59	
60	    work('w:bres', ['Bresnahan', 'Brynjolfsson'], 2002, 'The Quarterly Journal of Economics', '10.1/b')
61	    expr('e:bres:j', 'w:bres', 'journal_version')
62	    manif('m:bres', 'e:bres:j', 'w:bres', BRES_SPAN, 'journal_article')
63	
64	    work('w:autor', ['Autor', 'Levy', 'Murnane'], 2003, 'The Quarterly Journal of Economics', '10.1/a')
65	    expr('e:autor:j', 'w:autor', 'journal_version')
66	    manif('m:autor', 'e:autor:j', 'w:autor', AUTOR_SPAN, 'journal_article')
67	
68	    # A REAL JOURNAL ARTICLE whose span contains 10.25 — the substring that leaks a fabricated "0.2".
69	    work('w:leak', ['Stiroh'], 2002, 'American Economic Review', '10.1/l')
70	    expr('e:leak:j', 'w:leak', 'journal_version')
71	    manif('m:leak', 'e:leak:j', 'w:leak', LEAK_SPAN, 'journal_article')
72	
73	    # ---- THE P0, AS A FIXTURE ----------------------------------------------------------------------
74	    # The metadata row says "Journal of Political Economy". THE BYTES ARE NBER WORKING PAPER 23285.
75	    # Peer review changed this number: 0.37pp in the working paper, 0.2pp in the published JPE. The
76	    # journal expression EXISTS — and we do not hold one byte of it.
77	    work('w:ar', ['Acemoglu', 'Restrepo'], 2020, 'Journal of Political Economy', '10.1086/705716')
78	    expr('e:ar:wp', 'w:ar', 'working_paper')
79	    expr('e:ar:j', 'w:ar', 'journal_version')
80	    manif('m:ar', 'e:ar:wp', 'w:ar', AR_SPAN, 'working_paper')
81	
82	    def card(cid, mid, span, claim, **kw):
83	        m = g.manifestations[mid]
84	        s = m.text.index(span)
85	        b = g.bind_span(mid, s, s + len(span))
86	        att = g.resolve_attribution(mid, P.JOURNAL_ONLY)
87	        w = g.works[m.work_id]
88	        return dict(
89	            id=cid, manifestation_id=mid, content_hash=b['content_hash'],
90	            span_start=s, span_end=s + len(span), span_raw=b['text'], span=span, claim=claim,
91	            expression_id=b['expression_id'],
92	            permitted_expression_ids=list(b['permitted_expression_ids']),
93	            attribution_target_expression_id=att.names_expression_id,
94	            work_id=m.work_id, evidence_unit_id=m.work_id, authors=w.authors, year=w.year,
95	            venue=w.venue, level=kw.get('level', 'firm'), horizon='long-run',
96	            method='observational', mechanisms=kw.get('mech', []), corroborating_sources=[],
97	            source_version=m.content_hash[:12], text_field='fulltext')
98	
99	    cards = [
100	        card('c:bres', 'm:bres', BRES_SPAN,
101	             'Computer automation of routine work has been limited in scope.'),
102	        card('c:autor', 'm:autor', AUTOR_SPAN,
103	             'Computer capital substitutes for workers in routine tasks.',
104	             level='occupation', mech=['task displacement']),
105	        card('c:leak', 'm:leak', LEAK_SPAN, 'Productivity grew 10.25 percent.'),
106	        card('c:ar', 'm:ar', AR_SPAN,
107	             'Employment falls 0.2 percentage points per robot per thousand workers.', level='region'),
108	    ]
109	    return g, cards
110

"""
OpenRouter LLM Client for polaris graph.

Single gateway via OpenRouter. Default model = OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL
(default deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro per I-cd-009 / GH#624 Carney demo lock).
Two call modes:
- reason(): reasoning ON, returned separately in reasoning_details
- generate(): reasoning OFF, clean prose output only

No CoT scrubbing. No regex filters. No post-processing hacks.
The API handles separation at the protocol level.
"""

import asyncio
import json
import logging
import os
import re
import threading
import time
import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Optional, Type, TypeVar

import httpx
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from pydantic import BaseModel, ValidationError

from src.polaris_graph.tracing import _current_tracer

# all calls are gate-flagged via is_active() so the hot path pays one contextvar read).
from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb_capture
# B10 (2026-06-14): token-limit resolver — module-top import (stdlib-only module, no
# circular import; same `from src.polaris_graph...` convention as the imports above).
# Imported here (not lazily inside _call_impl) so an import failure is LOUD at module
# load — never silently swallowed by the call-site guard, which would leave the
# qwen-judge HTTP-400 unfixed (Codex B10 diff-gate iter-1 P1).
from src.polaris_graph.llm.token_limit_resolver import (
    compute_allowed_max_tokens as _resolve_allowed_max_tokens,
    estimate_prompt_tokens as _estimate_prompt_tokens,
)

load_dotenv()

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

T = TypeVar("T", bound=BaseModel)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# FIX-305: Persistent cost ledger
_COST_LEDGER_PATH = Path(os.getenv("PG_COST_LEDGER_PATH", "logs/pg_cost_ledger.jsonl"))

OPENROUTER_API_KEY = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "")
OPENROUTER_BASE_URL = os.getenv(
    "OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
)
# I-beatboth-008 (#1285): all-GLM-5.2 -> the no-model-given default tracks the lock's generator
# fallback (env_vars.generator.fallbacks=[OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL]); deepseek -> z-ai/glm-5.2.
OPENROUTER_MODEL = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_DEFAULT_MODEL", "z-ai/glm-5.2")
OPENROUTER_BUDGET_USD = float(os.getenv("OPENROUTER_BUDGET_USD", "50.0"))

# R-2 (readiness gate): hard per-run cost cap — a RUNAWAY-LOOP GUARD,
# not an economic limit. Measured by cumulative cost across ALL
# OpenRouterClient instances within a single run.
#
# I-gen-003 (2026-05-14): raised the default from $0.10 to $10.00.
# The $0.10 default was tuned for the V3.2-Exp generator
# ($0.005-$0.01/run). DeepSeek V4 Pro is reasoning-first — it emits
# ~3x the tokens (huge reasoning traces: ~5000 reasoning tokens/call
# observed) and the I-gen-003 CoT-recovery regen loop adds up to 3
# extra section calls. Under the stale $0.10 cap a normal V4 Pro run
# trips BudgetExceededError before it can finish — i.e. the guard
# false-fires on the new generator's NORMAL cost profile. $10.00
# matches the honest_sweep_job_runner.py default and still catches a genuine
# infinite loop / recursive-outline runaway. Override per-run via the

content.content:
612	class GraphIntegrityError(ValueError):
613	    """A graph on disk that does not agree with itself. It is REFUSED, never repaired."""
614	
615	
616	# What it takes to ASSERT that a span survives from one expression into another. Only a claim about
617	# BYTES can do it -- that is the entire content of the word "span-preserving".
618	_AUTHENTICATES = re.compile(
619	    r'byte[- ]for[- ]byte|byte[- ]level|checksum|sha-?256|content[_ -]hash'
620	    r'|identical (?:bytes|text|spans?)|span[- ]level (?:diff|comparison)'
621	    r'|(?:diffed|compared) (?:it )?against the .{0,30}(?:pdf|bytes|text|version)', re.I)
622	_PROVES_NOTHING_ABOUT_BYTES = re.compile(
623	    r'\btitles?\b|\bmetadata\b|\bsimilar\b|\bplausib|\blooks? like\b|self[- ]describ|\bassum', re.I)
624	
625	
626	def authentication_failure(basis: str) -> str:
627	    """'' if `basis` authenticates SPAN PRESERVATION; otherwise the reason it does not.
628	
629	    Sol, verbatim: "A title similarity match can PROPOSE predecessor_of; it CANNOT ASSERT
630	    exact_copy_of."
631	
632	    An ASSERTED span-preserving edge is the ONLY thing in this graph that lets a span name a document
633	    WHOSE BYTES WE DO NOT HOLD. It is therefore the only thing worth attacking, and it was guarded on
634	    ONE of its two types: the old check read `type == 'exact_copy_of' and 'title' in basis`. But
635	    `accepted_manuscript_of` is span-preserving too, and it widens attribution identically — so
636	
637	        add_edge(wp, journal, 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'ASSERTED', 'the titles match')
638	
639	    was ACCEPTED, and it made the working paper's spans journal-attributable. The rule is not "no
640	    title matches for exact_copy_of". The rule is: an ASSERTED span-preserving edge must name
641	    BYTE-LEVEL EVIDENCE, whatever it is called. Both directions are enforced here, in ONE predicate,
642	    used by `add_edge()` at construction AND by `from_json()` at load — because an edge that cannot be
643	    constructed but CAN BE LOADED is not a rule, it is a speed bump.
644	    """
645	    if not basis or not basis.strip():
646	        return 'an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it'
647	    weak = _PROVES_NOTHING_ABOUT_BYTES.search(basis)
648	    if weak:
649	        return (f'the basis rests on {weak.group(0)!r}, which proves NOTHING ABOUT BYTES — PEER REVIEW '
650	                f'CHANGES NUMBERS, and matching titles/metadata cannot show a span survived it')
651	    if not _AUTHENTICATES.search(basis):
652	        return ('the basis names no BYTE-LEVEL evidence (a byte-for-byte / checksum / span-level '
653	                'comparison against the target expression). Nothing else can ASSERT span preservation')
654	    return ''
655	
656	
657	@dataclass(frozen=True)
658	class SourcePolicy:
659	    """WHICH EXPRESSIONS THIS ANSWER IS ALLOWED TO CITE. The task's instruction, made mechanical.
660	
661	    This is an ARGUMENT, not a stored field, because admissibility is a property of THE ANSWER and not
662	    of the bytes. The same accepted manuscript is inadmissible under "journal articles only" and
663	    admissible under "any identified version"; a boolean on the node has to be wrong for one of them.
664	    `Expression.citable_in_journal_only_answer` was exactly that boolean, and it is gone.
665	    """
666	    name: str
667	    permitted_expression_kinds: tuple[str, ...]
668	    require_complete: bool = True
669	
670	
671	#: Task 72. "If only the working paper is available, citing it VIOLATES THE JOURNAL-ONLY INSTRUCTION."
672	JOURNAL_ONLY  = SourcePolicy('journal_articles_only', ('journal_version',))
673	PEER_REVIEWED = SourcePolicy('peer_reviewed', ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version'))
674	#: For a legal or clinical question, the authoritative text IS the source. There is no journal here.
675	OFFICIAL_TEXT = SourcePolicy('official_text_only', ('official_text', 'registry_record'))
676	ANY_VERSION   = SourcePolicy('any_identified_version',
677	                             ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version', 'accepted_manuscript',
678	                              'working_paper', 'preprint', 'official_text', 'registry_record'))
679	
680	
681	@dataclass(frozen=True)
682	class Attribution:
683	    """The resolved answer to: may a span from these bytes be attributed AT ALL, and TO WHAT?"""
684	    manifestation_id: str
685	    content_hash: str
686	    expression_id: str                        # the expression whose bytes these ARE
687	    permitted_expression_ids: tuple[str, ...]  # everything a span here may name (IDs — never prose)
688	    policy: str
689	    admitted: bool
690	    names_expression_id: str | None           # THE id the sentence must name. None => name NOTHING.
691	    text: str | None                          # display cache. NOTHING IS EVER VALIDATED AGAINST IT.
692	    refusal: str | None
693	
694	
695	@dataclass
696	class Graph:
697	    works: dict[str, Work] = field(default_factory=dict)
698	    expressions: dict[str, Expression] = field(default_factory=dict)
699	    manifestations: dict[str, Manifestation] = field(default_factory=dict)
700	    edges: list[Edge] = field(default_factory=list)
701	
702	    # -- construction -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
703	    def add_edge(self, src: str, dst: str, type: str, status: str, basis: str) -> Edge:
704	        if type not in EDGE_TYPES:
705	            raise ValueError(f'unknown edge type {type!r}')
706	        if status not in ('PROPOSED', 'ASSERTED'):
707	            raise ValueError(f'edge status must be PROPOSED or ASSERTED, not {status!r}')
708	        if not basis or not basis.strip():
709	            # An edge with no evidence is the entire failure we are here to end.
710	            raise ValueError('an edge MUST carry a basis naming the evidence for it')
711	        if status == 'ASSERTED' and type in SPAN_PRESERVING:
712	            # Enforced in the constructor so no caller can talk its way past it, and re-enforced in
713	            # from_json() so no FILE can either.
714	            why = authentication_failure(basis)
715	            if why:
716	                raise ValueError(f'cannot ASSERT {type} — {why}')
717	        e = Edge(src, dst, type, status, basis)
718	        self.edges.append(e)
719	        return e
720	
721	    # -- THE CORE RULE ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
722	    def attribution_targets(self, manifestation_id: str) -> list[str]:
723	        """Which expressions a span in this manifestation MAY name. Sol's rule, mechanised.
724	
725	        Always: the manifestation's own expression -- the bytes we hold are, definitionally, that
726	        expression's bytes. Additionally: any expression reachable by an ASSERTED, SPAN-PRESERVING
727	        edge, because such an edge is exactly a proof that the cited source contains the same span.
728	        Nothing else. A `predecessor_of` edge, however obvious, transfers NOTHING.
729	        """
730	        m = self.manifestations[manifestation_id]
731	        targets = [m.expression_id]
732	        for e in self.edges:
733	            if e.src == m.expression_id and e.type in SPAN_PRESERVING and e.status == 'ASSERTED':
734	                if e.dst not in targets:
735	                    targets.append(e.dst)
736	        return targets
737	
738	    # -- THE API. THREE FUNCTIONS. --------------------------------------------------------------
739	    def bind_span(self, manifestation_id: str, start: int, end: int) -> dict:
740	        """A span binds to its EXACT manifestation and content hash. This is what a card must carry
741	        instead of a bare DOI -- a DOI names a Work, and a Work has no bytes.
742	
743	        BOUNDS ARE VALIDATED HERE. THEY WERE NOT, AND PYTHON IS SILENT ABOUT NONSENSE: for a
744	        1,000-char text, `text[-5:20]`, `text[900:100]` and `text[5000:6000]` are all `''`. Every one
745	        of those is a span that BINDS CLEANLY AND THEN VERIFIES — its stored text is empty, the empty
746	        slice still equals it, and the content hash is perfectly correct. The card would name a real
747	        paper, carry a real hash, pass verify_span(), and quote NOTHING FROM IT. That is a fabricated
748	        citation with a valid receipt, which is the exact shape of every defect in this file's header.
749	        A negative offset is worse than empty: `text[-5:]` silently indexes from the END and binds a
750	        span from somewhere else entirely.
751	
752	        So they are REFUSED, not clamped. Clamping would invent a span the miner never mined.
753	
754	        RETURNS IDENTIFIERS, NOT PROSE. `may_name` used to hand back English SENTENCES, so a consumer
755	        deciding what was legal had to string-match a display string. Prose is a cache; an id resolves.
756	        """
757	        m = self.manifestations.get(manifestation_id)
758	        if m is None:
759	            raise SpanBindingError(f'no manifestation {manifestation_id!r} — a span cannot bind to '
760	                                   f'bytes we do not hold')
761	        for nm, v in (('start', start), ('end', end)):
762	            if isinstance(v, bool) or not isinstance(v, int):
763	                raise SpanBindingError(f'{nm} offset must be an int, got {v!r} ({type(v).__name__})')
764	        n = len(m.text)
765	        if start < 0 or end < 0:
766	            raise SpanBindingError(
767	                f'NEGATIVE span [{start}:{end}] on {manifestation_id} — Python would index from the '
768	                f'END of the text and bind a span from a different part of the document')
769	        if end <= start:
770	            raise SpanBindingError(
771	                f'EMPTY OR REVERSED span [{start}:{end}] on {manifestation_id} — it slices to "" and '
772	                f'would then verify perfectly against nothing, under a real citation')
773	        if end > n:
774	            raise SpanBindingError(
775	                f'span [{start}:{end}] runs past the end of {manifestation_id} ({n} chars) — Python '
776	                f'would truncate it silently, and the stored text would not be the span that was mined')
777	        return dict(
778	            manifestation_id=manifestation_id,
779	            content_hash=m.content_hash,
780	            span_start=start, span_end=end,
781	            text=m.text[start:end],
782	            expression_id=m.expression_id,
783	            permitted_expression_ids=list(self.attribution_targets(manifestation_id)),
784	        )
785	
786	    def resolve_attribution(self, manifestation_id: str,
787	                            source_policy: SourcePolicy = JOURNAL_ONLY) -> Attribution:
788	        """MAY a span from these bytes be attributed under THIS task's instruction, and TO WHAT ID?
789	
790	        Two independent conditions, BOTH required:
791	          1. the bytes are usable as evidence at all — complete, uncorrupted, the right work; AND
792	          2. some expression the span MAY LEGALLY NAME (own expression, or one across an ASSERTED
793	             span-preserving edge) is of a kind THIS POLICY PERMITS.
794	
795	        A span from a corrupt PDF of the real journal article is still inadmissible -- see Weiss, whose
796	        verbatim span said "633 per cent" because the PDF was a Caesar-shifted encoding of "300".
797	        """
798	        m = self.manifestations.get(manifestation_id)
799	        if m is None:
800	            raise KeyError(f'no manifestation {manifestation_id!r}')
801	        targets = tuple(self.attribution_targets(manifestation_id))
802	        base = dict(manifestation_id=manifestation_id, content_hash=m.content_hash,
803	                    expression_id=m.expression_id, permitted_expression_ids=targets,
804	                    policy=source_policy.name)
805	
806	        if source_policy.require_complete and not m.profile.get('complete'):
807	            why = '; '.join(m.profile.get('incomplete_because') or ['the bytes are not a usable document'])
808	            return Attribution(**base, admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None,
809	                               refusal=f'these bytes may not carry a finding: {why}')
810	
811	        # Prefer the expression whose bytes these ACTUALLY ARE. Widening is a fallback, never a first
812	        # choice: naming the document we hold is always the strongest true thing we can say.
813	        permitted = [t for t in targets
814	                     if self.expressions[t].kind in source_policy.permitted_expression_kinds]
815	        if not permitted:
816	            kinds = ', '.join(sorted({self.expressions[t].kind for t in targets})) or 'nothing'
817	            return Attribution(
818	                **base, admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None,
819	                refusal=(f'policy `{source_policy.name}` permits '
820	                         f'{{{", ".join(source_policy.permitted_expression_kinds)}}}, and a span here '
821	                         f'may only name {{{kinds}}}. No ASSERTED span-preserving edge widens it — '
822	                         f'citing a permitted source with THESE bytes would be fabrication'))
823	        chosen = m.expression_id if m.expression_id in permitted else permitted[0]
824	        return Attribution(**base, admitted=True, names_expression_id=chosen,
825	                           text=self.expressions[chosen].attribution, refusal=None)
826	
827	    def verify_span(self, binding: dict) -> bool:
828	        """The enforcement point. A binding arriving here is AN UNTRUSTED INPUT — by now it has been
829	        through a JSON file — so this re-checks EVERYTHING bind_span() checked, and then the bytes.
830	
831	        A gate that trusts the offsets it is handed is a gate that verifies `text[900:100] == ''`.
832	        """
833	        try:
834	            mid = binding['manifestation_id']
835	            s, e = binding['span_start'], binding['span_end']
836	            stored, chash = binding['text'], binding['content_hash']
837	        except (KeyError, TypeError):
838	            return False
839	        m = self.manifestations.get(mid)
840	        if m is None or m.content_hash != chash:
841	            return False
842	        for v in (s, e):
843	            if isinstance(v, bool) or not isinstance(v, int):
844	                return False
845	        if s < 0 or e <= s or e > len(m.text):
846	            return False
847	        # the node itself must not have been edited since it was loaded: the hash is over THE TEXT.
848	        if hashlib.sha256(m.text.encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest() != m.content_hash:
849	            return False
850	        if m.text[s:e] != stored:
851	            return False
852	        # ...and the permitted set is not a cache. If an edge changed, the STORED permission is stale,
853	        # and a stale permission is how a span keeps naming a journal it was never allowed to name.
854	        if 'permitted_expression_ids' in binding:
855	            if list(binding['permitted_expression_ids']) != list(self.attribution_targets(mid)):
856	                return False
857	        return True
858	
859	    # -- derived conveniences (they go THROUGH the API above; they are not a second door) ----------
860	    def journal_attributable(self, manifestation_id: str) -> bool:
861	        """May a span from these bytes be printed as a finding OF A JOURNAL ARTICLE? (task 72)"""
862	        return self.resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, JOURNAL_ONLY).admitted
863	
864	    # -- serialisation ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
865	    def to_json(self) -> dict:
866	        return dict(
867	            works=[asdict(w) for w in self.works.values()],
868	            expressions=[asdict(e) for e in self.expressions.values()],
869	            # text is retained on disk too: NOTHING IS EVER DELETED.
870	            manifestations=[asdict(m) for m in self.manifestations.values()],
871	            edges=[asdict(e) for e in self.edges],
872	        )
873	
874	    @classmethod
875	    def from_json(cls, data: dict | str) -> 'Graph':
876	        """STRICT LOAD. A GRAPH ON DISK IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT.
877	
878	        Every check here exists because the file it reads is the same KIND of artifact that lied to us
879	        already. `to_json()` writes the text, its hash, its word count and its derived profile, and any
880	        copy of that file — hand-edited, half-written, merged, or produced by an older version of this
881	        module — can disagree with itself in every one of those places. A loader that accepts what it
882	        is handed re-opens the hole FROM THE OTHER SIDE: the enforcement point would read a
883	        `complete: true` that no bytes support, and would enforce it.
884	
885	        REFUSES, NEVER REPAIRS:
886	          - a content_hash that is not sha256(text)                  [the span gate's only anchor]
887	          - an n_words that is not len(text.split())                 [the count that describes a
888	                                                                       document we no longer hold —
889	                                                                       the exact defect event_ledger
890	                                                                       catches in the live corpus]
891	          - a stored extractability verdict the text does not support
892	          - a `complete: true` (or false) THE REGISTRY DOES NOT DERIVE for that kind and that text
893	          - a manifestation naming an expression or a work that does not exist
894	          - a manifestation whose expression belongs to a DIFFERENT work
895	          - an expression naming a work that does not exist
896	          - an edge whose endpoints do not exist, or that carries no basis
897	          - an ASSERTED span-preserving edge whose basis does not AUTHENTICATE span preservation
898	          - any unknown field, anywhere (a schema drift silently drops the field a gate reads)
899	        """
900	        if isinstance(data, str):
901	            data = json.loads(data)
902	        g = cls()
903	        err: list[str] = []
904	
905	        def _fields(cl) -> set[str]:
906	            return set(cl.__dataclass_fields__)
907	
908	        def _take(cl, d: dict, what: str):
909	            extra = set(d) - _fields(cl)
910	            if extra:
911	                err.append(f'{what}: unknown field(s) {sorted(extra)} — this graph was not written by '
912	                           f'this schema, and a field a gate reads may be missing from it')
913	                return None
914	            missing = _fields(cl) - set(d) - {'profile', 'kind'}
915	            if missing:
916	                err.append(f'{what}: missing field(s) {sorted(missing)}')
917	                return None
918	            return cl(**d)
919	
920	        for d in data.get('works', []):
921	            w = _take(Work, d, f"work {d.get('id')!r}")
922	            if w is None:
923	                continue
924	            if w.kind not in WORK_KINDS:
925	                err.append(f'work {w.id!r}: kind {w.kind!r} is not a registered work kind {WORK_KINDS}')
926	            g.works[w.id] = w
927	
928	        for d in data.get('expressions', []):
929	            e = _take(Expression, d, f"expression {d.get('id')!r}")
930	            if e is None:
931	                continue
932	            if e.kind not in EXPRESSION_KINDS:
933	                err.append(f'expression {e.id!r}: kind {e.kind!r} is not in EXPRESSION_KINDS')
934	            if e.work_id not in g.works:
935	                err.append(f'expression {e.id!r}: names work {e.work_id!r}, WHICH DOES NOT EXIST')
936	            g.expressions[e.id] = e
937	
938	        for d in data.get('manifestations', []):
939	            m = _take(Manifestation, d, f"manifestation {d.get('id')!r}")
940	            if m is None:
941	                continue

content.text: The graph loader is strict (refuses hash/word-count mismatches at load). So "wrong hash" poison must live in the **card**, not the manifestation. Now the CardBundle + validator contract.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE TYPED REPORT AST — THE LAW, MECHANISED. There is no other way to say a sentence.
3	
4	    Every sentence is ATTRIBUTED (names a source -> MUST be entailed by THAT source's VERBATIM SPAN)
5	    or OWNED (reviewer's voice -> names no source, carries no new particular, MAY be non-entailed).
6	    THE VERBATIM SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE. The model-written `claim` is a display cache; nothing is
7	    ever validated against it.
8	
9	WHY THIS FILE EXISTS, AND WHY IT IS NOT ANOTHER PREFLIGHT
10	---------------------------------------------------------
11	Six adversary attacks succeeded while `test_gate_is_wired.py` was 16/16 GREEN and nothing had been
12	weakened. The reason was not a weak check. It was that **the checks certified a lane the fabrication no
13	longer used**: 6,463 lines of correct, self-tested modules were dead code, and the composer imported not
14	one of them. `provenance.py` passed its own 18/18 self-test while the P0 it was built to stop sat live on
15	disk.
16	
17	So the gate is no longer a function the writer is asked to call. IT IS THE ONLY TYPE A SENTENCE CAN HAVE.
18	A sentence that has not been through here is not "ungated" — IT DOES NOT EXIST, because prose is never
19	concatenated anywhere in this pipeline. `render()` is the only thing that emits characters, it emits them
20	only from validated nodes, and `publisher.py` is the only thing that may write them into the release
21	directory.
22	
23	WHAT CHANGED IN THE SHAPE OF A SENTENCE
24	---------------------------------------
25	The old composer inferred WHO A SENTENCE WAS ABOUT BY LOOKING FOR SURNAMES IN IT (`_cited_cards`). That
26	is how "Bresnahan reports task displacement" — a real mechanism, a real paper, a FABRICATED BINDING —
27	was gated against whichever card the regex happened to hit first. Sol: **DO NOT INFER VOICE OR SOURCE
28	IDENTITY FROM SURNAMES.**
29	
30	Here, the model does not write attribution prose AT ALL, and it does not get to imply a source by naming
31	one. It emits a bare FINDING plus THE CARD ID it came from:
32	
33	    Attributed(clauses=[Clause(card_id='...:76927-77299', text='the employment-to-population ratio
34	                               falls by 0.37 percentage points per robot per thousand workers')])
35	
36	and THIS FILE renders the attribution — from the graph, from the expression the SOURCE POLICY selected.
37	The model cannot copy an attribution, cannot invent one, and cannot name a journal whose bytes we do not
38	hold, because it never types the journal's name.
39	
40	A CROSS-SOURCE SENTENCE IS A CONJUNCTION OF ATTRIBUTED CLAUSES, EACH BOUND TO ITS OWN CARD. That keeps
41	the heaviest criterion on the board (critical synthesis, w=0.0800) reachable — the old gate deleted every
42	comparative sentence — while the fabricated binding still dies, because the clause that says "task
43	displacement" names Bresnahan's card_id and 'task displacement' is not in Bresnahan's span.
44	
45	THE CHAIN, WHICH IS THE WHOLE POINT
46	-----------------------------------
47	    sentence -> card -> bound span -> manifestation_id + content_hash -> permitted expression -> attribution
48	
49	`CardBundle.resolve()` IS that chain, as one function, and it is the only door. Nothing in this file, in
50	`cellcog_composer.py`, or in `publisher.py` reads a card's `attribution`, `venue`, `doi` or `authors` to
51	decide anything. Those are display caches. The graph decides.
52	"""
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import hashlib
56	import re
57	import sys
58	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
59	from pathlib import Path
60	
61	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
62	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
63	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
64	
65	import provenance as P  # noqa: E402
66	from synthesis_contract import Premise, Synthesis, validate, OPERATIONS  # noqa: E402
67	
68	
69	# =================================================================================================
70	# 0. THE NODES. A report is a list of these and NOTHING ELSE.
71	# =================================================================================================
72	
73	@dataclass(frozen=True)
74	class Clause:
75	    """ONE finding, from ONE card. The text is the FINDING ONLY — no attribution, no journal, no year.
76	
77	    `card_id` is not a hint and it is not decoration: it is the sentence's ONLY connection to reality,
78	    and it is checked against the bytes. The model may not name a source in `text`; if it does, the
79	    clause is refused (SOURCE_NAMED_IN_CLAUSE_TEXT), because a named source in model prose is a source
80	    WE DID NOT RESOLVE.
81	    """
82	    card_id: str
83	    text: str
84	
85	
86	#: How clause i>0 joins the clause before it. A CLOSED SET — the model may not write connective prose,
87	#: because a connective is where an unsourced particular hides ("..., which explains why 40% of...").
88	CONNECTIVES = ('while', 'whereas', 'by contrast', 'and', 'though', 'but', 'yet')
89	
90	
91	@dataclass(frozen=True)
92	class Attributed:
93	    """A sentence that names sources. EVERY clause is entailed by ITS OWN card's verbatim span."""
94	    clauses: tuple[Clause, ...]
95	    connective: str = 'while'
96	
97	    def card_ids(self) -> tuple[str, ...]:
98	        return tuple(c.card_id for c in self.clauses)
99	
100	
101	@dataclass(frozen=True)
102	class Owned:
103	    """The reviewer's voice. MAY BE NON-ENTAILED — that is what insight IS.
104	
105	    It must name NO source and carry NO new particular. Two regimes, one type:
106	
107	      * `premise_ids` non-empty  -> a SYNTHESIS over admitted premises. It must additionally pass a
108	        typed operation in `synthesis_contract` (a mechanism may appear ONLY if a premise states it).
109	        This is the body lane.
110	      * `premise_ids` empty      -> a FRAME sentence (an abstract's "Objective.", a section's bridge).
111	        It is licensed by nothing and may therefore assert nothing particular: no number, no source, no
112	        proper noun the review has not already earned. This is the lane the hand-written abstract used
113	        to bypass the law through, and it is now a TYPE with a CHECK, not an f-string in write_report().
114	    """
115	    text: str
116	    premise_ids: tuple[str, ...] = ()
117	
118	
119	@dataclass(frozen=True)
120	class Heading:
121	    level: int
122	    text: str
123	
124	
125	@dataclass(frozen=True)
126	class ParagraphBreak:
127	    """A paragraph boundary, AS A NODE — so a paragraph cannot be made by concatenating a '\\n\\n'.
128	
129	    If whitespace were prose, the writer would have a lane that emits characters without a type, and
130	    every hole in this file's header began as a lane that emitted characters without a type.
131	    """
132	
133	
134	@dataclass(frozen=True)
135	class EvidenceTable:
136	    """Every cell is a field of a card whose binding RE-VERIFIES at render time. No model prose."""
137	    card_ids: tuple[str, ...]
138	    caption: str = ('**Table 1. Quantitative findings across the reviewed literature.** Each row reports '
139	                    'a figure stated by the cited paper itself, verified against that paper\'s own bytes.')
140	
141	
142	Node = Attributed | Owned | Heading | ParagraphBreak | EvidenceTable
143	
144	
145	# =================================================================================================
146	# 1. THE CHAIN.  sentence -> card -> bound span -> manifestation + hash -> permitted expression
147	# =================================================================================================
148	
149	@dataclass(frozen=True)
150	class Resolution:
151	    ok: bool
152	    card_id: str
153	    refusal: str = ''
154	    manifestation_id: str = ''
155	    content_hash: str = ''
156	    expression_id: str = ''            # the expression whose bytes the span ACTUALLY came from
157	    names_expression_id: str = ''      # the expression the sentence is PERMITTED to name
158	    span: str = ''                     # THE VERBATIM SPAN. THE ONLY EVIDENCE.
159	    span_start: int = -1
160	    span_end: int = -1
161	    work_id: str = ''
162	    attribution: str = ''              # RENDERED FROM THE GRAPH. Never copied off the card.
163	    card: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
164	
165	
166	def _binding_from_card(c: dict) -> dict:
167	    """The binding a card carries, in the shape `Graph.verify_span()` demands.
168	
169	    A card that has not been through `graph.bind_span()` HAS NO BINDING, and this returns {} rather than
170	    a plausible-looking dict assembled from a DOI and two integers. That distinction is the P0: a DOI
171	    names a WORK, and a work has no bytes.
172	    """
173	    if not c.get('manifestation_id') or not c.get('content_hash'):
174	        return {}
175	    for k in ('span_start', 'span_end', 'span_raw'):
176	        if c.get(k) is None:
177	            return {}
178	    return dict(
179	        manifestation_id=c['manifestation_id'],
180	        content_hash=c['content_hash'],
181	        span_start=c['span_start'],
182	        span_end=c['span_end'],
183	        text=c['span_raw'],
184	        permitted_expression_ids=list(c.get('permitted_expression_ids') or []),
185	    )
186	
187	
188	class CardBundle:
189	    """ONE card lane. ONE graph. ONE policy. THE SEAM IS GONE.
190	
191	    There were TWO DISCONNECTED CARD LANES: the miner wrote `evidence_cards_v2.json` and the composer
192	    read `evidence_cards.json` — a file whose cards carry no manifestation, no hash and no expression,
193	    and whose four Frey & Osborne cards were mined out of an ORA LANDING PAGE. The composer could not
194	    have detected that, because the lane it read had nothing in it to detect with.
195	
196	    A bundle is constructed from an EXPLICIT path plus the graph, and it REFUSES to exist if the bytes
197	    on disk are not the bytes it was promised.
198	    """
199	
200	    def __init__(self, cards: list[dict], graph: P.Graph,
201	                 policy: P.SourcePolicy = P.JOURNAL_ONLY,
202	                 cards_sha: str = '', graph_sha: str = '', ledger_sha: str = ''):
203	        self.graph = graph
204	        self.policy = policy
205	        self.cards_sha = cards_sha
206	        self.graph_sha = graph_sha
207	        self.ledger_sha = ledger_sha
208	        # A DUPLICATE CARD ID IS A REFUSAL, NOT AN OVERWRITE.
209	        #
210	        # This dict used to be filled with `self.cards[cid] = c`, and the miner's id is
211	        # `{doi}:{span_start}-{span_end}` — so TWO FINDINGS MINED FROM ONE SPAN COLLIDE. On the first
212	        # real bundle, 13 of 52 cards were silently overwritten: the composer printed "52 cards, 0
213	        # refused" and had 39. The evidence did not fail a check. IT LEFT WITHOUT ONE, which is the
214	        # quieter half of every defect in this file's header. And a sentence citing a colliding id
215	        # names an AMBIGUOUS card: same span, different claim, different act, different table row.
216	        self.cards: dict[str, dict] = {}
217	        dupes: dict[str, int] = {}
218	        for c in cards:
219	            cid = c.get('id')
220	            if not cid:
221	                raise ValueError('a card with no `id` cannot be cited by a sentence, and is REFUSED')
222	            if cid in self.cards:
223	                dupes[cid] = dupes.get(cid, 1) + 1
224	                continue
225	            self.cards[cid] = c
226	        if dupes:
227	            n = sum(dupes.values()) - len(dupes)
228	            raise ValueError(
229	                f'{len(dupes)} DUPLICATE CARD ID(S) ({n} cards would be silently dropped) — a bundle '
230	                f'whose ids are ambiguous is not a bundle, and a sentence citing one of them names no '
231	                f'determinate evidence. Offenders: {sorted(dupes)[:3]}')
232	        self._cache: dict[str, Resolution] = {}
233	        # Every surname and venue the bundle knows. Used ONLY NEGATIVELY — to refuse a source name in
234	        # a lane that is not allowed to have one. We never infer WHICH source from a surname again.
235	        self._source_words: set[str] = set()
236	        for c in self.cards.values():
237	            for a in (c.get('authors') or []):
238	                if len(a) >= 4:
239	                    self._source_words.add(a.lower())
240	        for w in graph.works.values():
241	            for a in (w.authors or []):
242	                if len(a) >= 4:
243	                    self._source_words.add(a.lower())
244	
245	    # -- THE ONLY DOOR ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
246	    def resolve(self, card_id: str) -> Resolution:
247	        """sentence -> card -> bound span -> manifestation_id + content_hash -> permitted expression
248	        -> attribution.
249	
250	        EVERY step is re-derived from the graph HERE, at use time. Nothing stored on the card is
251	        trusted: not the attribution, not the permitted set, not the expression, not the hash.
252	
253	        `verify_span()` is called FIRST — before any field of the card is read for any purpose — because
254	        a card whose bytes do not verify is not a weak card, it is NOT A CARD, and reading its `venue`
255	        to make a decision is how a landing page became a peer-reviewed article.
256	        """
257	        if card_id in self._cache:
258	            return self._cache[card_id]
259	        r = self._resolve(card_id)
260	        self._cache[card_id] = r
261	        return r
262	
263	    def _resolve(self, card_id: str) -> Resolution:
264	        c = self.cards.get(card_id)
265	        if c is None:
266	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal='NO_SUCH_CARD_IN_BUNDLE')
267	
268	        binding = _binding_from_card(c)
269	        if not binding:
270	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal='CARD_IS_UNBOUND (no manifestation_id/content_hash '
271	                                                      '/span offsets — a DOI names a work, and a work has '
272	                                                      'no bytes)')
273	        # ---- 1. THE BYTES. Untrusted input; re-check offsets, hash, and the text itself.
274	        if not self.graph.verify_span(binding):
275	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal=f'SPAN_DOES_NOT_VERIFY against '
276	                                                      f'{binding["manifestation_id"]}')
277	        mid = binding['manifestation_id']
278	        m = self.graph.manifestations[mid]
279	
280	        # ---- 2. THE POLICY. What, if anything, may a span in these bytes NAME?
281	        att = self.graph.resolve_attribution(mid, self.policy)
282	        if not att.admitted:
283	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal=f'SOURCE_POLICY_REFUSES: {att.refusal}')
284	
285	        # ---- 3. THE CARD'S OWN STORED TARGET MUST BE THE ONE THE GRAPH RESOLVES *NOW*.
286	        #         A stored target is a cache, and a cache that is not checked is how a stale permission
287	        #         keeps naming a journal it was never allowed to name.
288	        stored = c.get('attribution_target_expression_id')
289	        if stored and stored != att.names_expression_id:
290	            return Resolution(False, card_id,
291	                              refusal=f'STALE_ATTRIBUTION_TARGET: card says {stored!r}, the graph '
292	                                      f'resolves {att.names_expression_id!r} under `{self.policy.name}`')
293	
294	        # ---- 4. THE ATTRIBUTION IS RENDERED FROM THE GRAPH. The card's own string is never read.
295	        expr = self.graph.expressions[att.names_expression_id]
296	        work = self.graph.works[expr.work_id]
297	        prose = render_attribution(work, expr)
298	        if not prose:
299	            return Resolution(False, card_id,
300	                              refusal=f'EXPRESSION_KIND_IS_NOT_RENDERABLE_AS_A_CITATION: {expr.kind!r}')
301	
302	        return Resolution(
303	            True, card_id, manifestation_id=mid, content_hash=binding['content_hash'],
304	            expression_id=binding.get('expression_id') or m.expression_id,
305	            names_expression_id=att.names_expression_id,
306	            span=binding['text'], span_start=binding['span_start'], span_end=binding['span_end'],
307	            work_id=m.work_id, attribution=prose, card=c)
308	
309	    def admitted_ids(self) -> list[str]:
310	        return [cid for cid in self.cards if self.resolve(cid).ok]
311	
312	    def names_a_source(self, text: str) -> str:
313	        """The surname/venue check, used ONLY to REFUSE. Returns the offending token, or ''.
314	
315	        This is not `_cited_cards()` coming back in through a window. That function asked "WHICH source
316	        is this sentence about?" and answered with a guess, then gated the sentence against the guess.
317	        This one asks "does this text name ANY source at all?" and uses the answer only to say NO. There
318	        is no lane in which a surname selects a card.
319	        """
320	        low = text.lower()
321	        for w in self._source_words:
322	            if re.search(rf'\b{re.escape(w)}\b', low):
323	                return w
324	        return ''
325	
326	
327	# =================================================================================================
328	# 2. ATTRIBUTION, RENDERED FROM THE GRAPH — NEVER WRITTEN OR COPIED BY A MODEL
329	# =================================================================================================
330	
331	#: The year is PROSE, never parenthetical: every one of the 281 "(YYYY)" parentheses in the reference
332	#: system is deleted by RACE's ArticleCleaner. `Expression.attribution` is the parenthetical display
333	#: form (`Acemoglu and Restrepo (2020), Journal of Political Economy`); it does not survive the cleaner,
334	#: so we re-render the SAME FACTS, from the SAME NODES, in the form that does.
335	_LEAD_FORMS = (
336	    'Writing in {venue_the} in {year}, {who} show that ',
337	    '{who}, writing in {venue_the} in {year}, report that ',
338	    'A {year} {venue} study by {who} finds that ',
339	    '{who} establish, in {venue_the} in {year}, that ',
340	    'The evidence {who} present in {venue_the} in {year} is that ',
341	)
342	_TRAIL_FORMS = (
343	    '{who}, writing in {venue_the} in {year}, report that ',
344	    'in {venue_the} in {year}, {who} show that ',
345	    '{who} find, in their {year} {venue} paper, that ',
346	)
347	
348	#: WHICH EXPRESSION KINDS MAY BE RENDERED AS A JOURNAL CITATION AT ALL.
349	#: A `working_paper` expression is NOT renderable here, ever, in any form. Under JOURNAL_ONLY it never
350	#: reaches this function (resolve_attribution refuses it first) — this is the SECOND lock on the same
351	#: door, and it exists because the first lock is the one that was found unlocked.
352	_JOURNAL_KINDS = ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version')
353	
354	
355	#: Venue names that take a definite article ("in the American Economic Review") as against those that do
356	#: not ("in PLOS ONE", "in Technovation", "in AI and Ethics"). Getting this wrong is not cosmetic: the
357	#: released artifact said "in the AI and Ethics in 2022", and a reviewer who writes that has not read
358	#: the journal. The rule is the head noun, not a hand-list of titles.
359	_TAKES_THE = re.compile(r'\b(journal|review|quarterly|proceedings|annals|bulletin|letters|transactions'
360	                        r'|magazine|gazette|record|reporter)\b', re.I)
361	
362	
363	def _venue_the(venue: str) -> str:
364	    """'the Journal of Political Economy', 'The Quarterly Journal of Economics', 'PLOS ONE'.
365	
366	    Never 'the The' (the venue already carries its article) and never 'the AI and Ethics'.
367	    """
368	    v = (venue or '').strip()
369	    if not v:
370	        return ''
371	    if v.lower().startswith('the '):
372	        return v
373	    return f'the {v}' if _TAKES_THE.search(v) else v
374	
375	
376	def _who(work: P.Work) -> str:
377	    a = [x for x in (work.authors or []) if x]
378	    if not a:
379	        return ''
380	    if len(a) == 1:
381	        return a[0]
382	    if len(a) == 2:
383	        return f'{a[0]} and {a[1]}'
384	    return f'{a[0]} and colleagues'
385	
386	
387	def render_attribution(work: P.Work, expr: P.Expression, lead: bool = True, form: int = 0) -> str:
388	    """The attribution clause for THIS expression. Returns '' if this expression may not be cited.
389	
390	    THE MODEL NEVER SEES THIS STRING BEFORE IT WRITES, AND NEVER TYPES IT. It writes a bare finding and
391	    a card id; the prose below is attached afterwards, from the work and the expression the policy
392	    chose. That is why "Writing in the Journal of Political Economy..." can no longer appear over bytes
393	    that came from NBER Working Paper 23285: the sentence's journal name is not something the model can
394	    produce.
395	    """
396	    if expr.kind not in _JOURNAL_KINDS:
397	        return ''
398	    who, venue = _who(work), (work.venue or '').strip()
399	    if not who or not venue or not work.year:
400	        return ''
401	    forms = _LEAD_FORMS if lead else _TRAIL_FORMS
402	    f = forms[form % len(forms)]
403	    return f.format(who=who, venue=venue, venue_the=_venue_the(venue), year=work.year)
404	
405	
406	# =================================================================================================
407	# 3. ENTAILMENT — AGAINST THE VERBATIM SPAN, AND NOTHING ELSE
408	# =================================================================================================
409	
410	_STOP = {'writing', 'article', 'journal', 'review', 'that', 'show', 'shows', 'find', 'finds', 'found',
411	         'report', 'reports', 'reported', 'demonstrate', 'demonstrates', 'evidence', 'study', 'their',
412	         'this', 'these', 'those', 'with', 'from', 'have', 'been', 'were', 'which', 'while', 'also',
413	         'than', 'when', 'more', 'most', 'such', 'they', 'them', 'about', 'paper', 'papers', 'result',
414	         'results', 'finding', 'findings', 'authors', 'author'}
415	
416	_NUM = re.compile(r'\d+(?:\.\d+)?')
417	
418	
419	def numbers_in(s: str) -> list[str]:
420	    return _NUM.findall(s or '')
421	
422	
423	def number_stands_alone(num: str, src: str) -> bool:
424	    """`"0.2" in "10.25"` IS TRUE. A substring test leaks a fabricated effect size through any source
425	    that happens to contain a longer number with the same digits in it — and we deliberately flood this
426	    prose with figures, which loads that hole with live rounds."""
427	    return bool(re.search(rf'(?<![\d.]){re.escape(num)}(?![\d])', src))
428	
429	
430	def entailed_by_span(text: str, span: str, work: P.Work | None = None,
431	                     min_overlap: float = 0.25) -> tuple[bool, str]:
432	    """Is THIS text supported by THIS verbatim span? The span is the only evidence there is.
433	
434	    `claim` — the model's restatement — is not a parameter of this function and never will be. The old
435	    gate read `src = f'{span} {claim}'`, and since the writer was handed only the claim, the chain was:
436	    the model writes the claim -> the writer writes from the claim -> the gate checks the writing
437	    against the claim. THE GATE VALIDATED THE MODEL AGAINST ITSELF.
438	    """
439	    src = (span or '').lower()
440	    if not src:
441	        return False, 'NO_SPAN'
442	    year = str(work.year) if work and work.year else ''
443	
444	    for num in numbers_in(text):
445	        if len(num) < 2 or num == year:
446	            continue
447	        if not number_stands_alone(num, src):
448	            return False, f'NUMBER_NOT_IN_SPAN:{num}'
449	
450	    words = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', (text or '').lower())} - _STOP
451	    if work:
452	        words -= {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', (work.venue or '').lower())}
453	        words -= {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ' '.join(work.authors or []).lower())}
454	    if not words:
455	        return True, ''
456	    src_words = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', src)}
457	    if len(words & src_words) / len(words) < min_overlap:
458	        return False, 'CONTENT_NOT_IN_SPAN'
459	    return True, ''
460	
461	
462	# =================================================================================================
463	# 4. VALIDATION — EVERY NODE, EVERY TIME, AT RENDER AND AGAIN AT PUBLISH
464	# =================================================================================================
465	
466	MARKER = re.compile(r'\[\d+\]')
467	PAREN_YEAR = re.compile(r'\((?:19|20)\d\d[a-z]?\)')
468	BANNED_META = re.compile(
469	    r'\b(this report|this review synthesi[sz]es|the pipeline|retrieved|the question above|'
470	    r'span-grounded|telemetry|our system|we retrieved|the corpus|evidence card)\b', re.I)
471	
472	
473	@dataclass
474	class Failure:
475	    node_index: int
476	    kind: str
477	    reason: str
478	    detail: str = ''
479	
480	    def __str__(self) -> str:
481	        d = f' :: {self.detail[:80]}' if self.detail else ''
482	        return f'[{self.node_index}] {self.kind}: {self.reason}{d}'
483	
484	
485	#: A sentence boundary: a terminator, whitespace, then a capital. Abbreviations ("et al.", "e.g.") and
486	#: initials are not boundaries — the old splitter amputated every attributed sentence at "et al." and
487	#: filled the report with stumps.
488	_ABBREV = re.compile(r'\b(et al|e\.g|i\.e|cf|vs|Dr|Prof|Mr|Mrs|Ms|St|Fig|No|pp|vol|ed|eds|approx|ca)\.$',
489	                     re.I)
490	_INITIAL = re.compile(r'\b[A-Z]\.$')
491	
492	
493	def split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
494	    out, buf = [], ''
495	    for chunk in re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])(\s+)', text or ''):
496	        buf += chunk
497	        if not chunk.strip():
498	            continue
499	        head = buf.strip()
500	        if _ABBREV.search(head) or _INITIAL.search(head):
501	            continue
502	        if re.search(r'[.!?]$', head):
503	            out.append(head)
504	            buf = ''
505	    if buf.strip():
506	        out.append(buf.strip())
507	    return [x for x in out if x]
508	
509	
510	def _common(text: str) -> str:
511	    """Refusals that apply in EVERY lane, in EVERY node, including the abstract."""
512	    if not (text or '').strip():
513	        return 'EMPTY'
514	    if MARKER.search(text):
515	        return 'CITATION_MARKER'          # deleted by the cleaner anyway; never write one
516	    if PAREN_YEAR.search(text):
517	        return 'PARENTHETICAL_YEAR'       # deleted by the cleaner; the year must be prose
518	    if BANNED_META.search(text):
519	        return 'META_COMMENTARY'
520	    # ---- ONE NODE, ONE SENTENCE. THE LAW IS PER-SENTENCE, SO THE TYPE MUST BE PER-SENTENCE.
521	    #
522	    # The publisher caught this on the first real release, and refused it. A node holding TWO sentences
523	    # gets ONE sentence_hash — the hash of the blob — so the sidecar has no receipt for either sentence
524	    # as it appears in the file, and `verify_release()` finds prose in the judged artifact that resolves
525	    # to NOTHING. The receipt has to be per-sentence because THE JUDGE READS SENTENCES.
526	    #
527	    # (The offender was my own hand-written Methods paragraph. The law caught its author again.)
528	    if len(split_sentences(text)) > 1:
529	        return 'NODE_HOLDS_MORE_THAN_ONE_SENTENCE'
530	    return ''
531	
532	
533	def validate_node(i: int, n: Node, b: CardBundle) -> list[Failure]:
534	    if isinstance(n, ParagraphBreak):
535	        return []
536	    if isinstance(n, Heading):
537	        return [] if (n.text or '').strip() else [Failure(i, 'Heading', 'EMPTY')]
538	
539	    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- ATTRIBUTED
540	    if isinstance(n, Attributed):
541	        f: list[Failure] = []
542	        if not n.clauses:
543	            return [Failure(i, 'Attributed', 'NO_CLAUSES')]
544	        if n.connective and n.connective not in CONNECTIVES:
545	            f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', 'CONNECTIVE_NOT_IN_CLOSED_SET', n.connective))
546	        for cl in n.clauses:
547	            why = _common(cl.text)
548	            if why:
549	                f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', why, cl.text[:70]))
550	                continue
551	            # THE CHAIN. If it does not resolve, the sentence does not exist.
552	            r = b.resolve(cl.card_id)
553	            if not r.ok:
554	                f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', r.refusal, cl.card_id))
555	                continue
556	            # THE MODEL MAY NOT NAME A SOURCE. It names a card_id; WE name the source.
557	            named = b.names_a_source(cl.text)
558	            if named:
559	                f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', 'SOURCE_NAMED_IN_CLAUSE_TEXT',
560	                                 f'{named!r} — attribution is rendered from the graph, not typed by '
561	                                 f'the model'))
562	                continue
563	            # ENTAILMENT, AGAINST THIS CLAUSE'S OWN CARD'S SPAN. This is the line that kills the
564	            # fabricated binding: the clause that says "task displacement" names Bresnahan's card,
565	            # and Bresnahan's span does not say it.
566	            work = b.graph.works.get(r.work_id)
567	            ok, why2 = entailed_by_span(cl.text, r.span, work)
568	            if not ok:
569	                f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', why2, f'{cl.card_id} :: {cl.text[:60]}'))
570	        return f
571	
572	    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- OWNED
573	    if isinstance(n, Owned):
574	        why = _common(n.text)
575	        if why:
576	            return [Failure(i, 'Owned', why, n.text[:70])]
577	        # 1. NAMES NO SOURCE.
578	        named = b.names_a_source(n.text)
579	        if named:
580	            return [Failure(i, 'Owned', 'OWNED_NAMES_A_SOURCE',
581	                            f'{named!r} — a sentence that names a source is ATTRIBUTED, and must be '
582	                            f'entailed by that source. It may not enter through the owned lane.')]
583	        # 2. CARRIES NO NEW PARTICULAR. A figure in the reviewer's own voice is sourced to nothing.
584	        if re.search(r'\d', n.text):
585	            return [Failure(i, 'Owned', 'OWNED_CARRIES_A_NUMBER', n.text[:70])]
586	        # 3. If it is licensed by premises, it must be a TYPED SYNTHESIS over them.
587	        if n.premise_ids:
588	            prem: dict[str, Premise] = {}
589	            for pid in n.premise_ids:
590	                r = b.resolve(pid)
591	                if not r.ok:
592	                    return [Failure(i, 'Owned', f'PREMISE_UNRESOLVED: {r.refusal}', pid)]
593	                c = r.card
594	                prem[pid] = Premise(
595	                    id=pid, text=r.span, source=r.attribution,
596	                    level=c.get('level', '') or c.get('unit_of_analysis', ''),
597	                    horizon=c.get('horizon', ''), method=c.get('method', '') or c.get('design', ''),
598	                    mechanisms=c.get('mechanisms') or [])
599	            if len(prem) < 2:
600	                return [Failure(i, 'Owned', 'SYNTHESIS_NEEDS_2_PREMISES', str(n.premise_ids))]
601	            for op in OPERATIONS:
602	                ok, _ = validate(Synthesis(op, list(prem), n.text), prem)
603	                if ok:
604	                    return []
605	            _, why2 = validate(Synthesis('CONTRASTS_LEVEL', list(prem), n.text), prem)
606	            return [Failure(i, 'Owned', f'SYNTHESIS_REFUSED:{why2}', n.text[:70])]
607	        # 4. A FRAME sentence: licensed by nothing, so it asserts nothing particular. Already checked
608	        #    for numbers and source names above. That is the whole of what the law permits it.
609	        return []
610	
611	    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- TABLE
612	    if isinstance(n, EvidenceTable):
613	        f = []
614	        for cid in n.card_ids:
615	            r = b.resolve(cid)
616	            if not r.ok:
617	                f.append(Failure(i, 'EvidenceTable', r.refusal, cid))
618	                continue
619	            # EVERY FIGURE IN THE ROW MUST STAND AS ITS OWN NUMBER IN THAT ROW'S OWN SPAN.
620	            claim = (r.card.get('claim') or '')
621	            work = b.graph.works.get(r.work_id)
622	            ok, why = entailed_by_span(claim, r.span, work, min_overlap=0.34)
623	            if not ok:
624	                f.append(Failure(i, 'EvidenceTable', f'ROW_{why}', f'{cid} :: {claim[:50]}'))
625	        return f
626	
627	    return [Failure(i, type(n).__name__, 'UNKNOWN_NODE_TYPE')]
628	
629	
630	def validate_report(nodes: list[Node], b: CardBundle) -> list[Failure]:
631	    out: list[Failure] = []
632	    for i, n in enumerate(nodes):
633	        out += validate_node(i, n, b)
634	    return out
635	
636	
637	# =================================================================================================
638	# 5. RENDER — THE ONLY PLACE PROSE IS EVER PRODUCED
639	# =================================================================================================
640	
641	def _fmt_sentence(n: Attributed, b: CardBundle, form_seed: int) -> str:
642	    parts = []
643	    for j, cl in enumerate(n.clauses):
644	        r = b.resolve(cl.card_id)
645	        expr = b.graph.expressions[r.names_expression_id]
646	        work = b.graph.works[expr.work_id]
647	        att = render_attribution(work, expr, lead=(j == 0), form=form_seed + j)
648	        body = cl.text.strip().rstrip('.')
649	        # lower-case the first letter of the finding: it follows "...show that".
650	        if body and body[0].isupper() and not body.startswith(tuple(w for w in ['AI', 'US', 'UK', 'EU'])):
651	            body = body[0].lower() + body[1:]
652	        parts.append((att + body) if j == 0 else f'{n.connective} {att}{body}')
653	    s = ', '.join(parts) if len(parts) > 1 else parts[0]
654	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip() + '.'
655	
656	
657	def _table(n: EvidenceTable, b: CardBundle, limit: int = 14) -> str:
658	    import html
659	    rows = []
660	    for cid in n.card_ids[:limit]:
661	        r = b.resolve(cid)
662	        c = r.card
663	        expr = b.graph.expressions[r.names_expression_id]
664	        work = b.graph.works[expr.work_id]
665	        who = _who(work)
666	        claim = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', html.unescape(c.get('claim') or '')).rstrip('.')
667	        if len(claim) > 155:
668	            claim = claim[:152].rsplit(' ', 1)[0] + '...'
669	        cell = lambda x: html.unescape(str(x or '--')).replace('|', '/')
670	        rows.append(f"| {cell(who)}, {work.year} | {cell(work.venue)[:34]} | "
671	                    f"{cell(c.get('level') or c.get('unit_of_analysis'))} | "
672	                    f"{cell(c.get('method') or c.get('design'))} | {claim} |")
673	    if len(rows) < 3:
674	        return ''
675	    return '\n'.join(['', n.caption, '',
676	                      '| Study | Journal | Level | Method | Quantitative finding |',
677	                      '|---|---|---|---|---|'] + rows)
678	
679	
680	def sentence_hash(s: str) -> str:
681	    """The sidecar's key. Normalised, so a whitespace edit to the released file is still resolvable —
682	    and a WORD edit is not."""
683	    return hashlib.sha256(re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s.strip()).encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
684	
685	
686	def render(nodes: list[Node], b: CardBundle) -> tuple[str, list[dict]]:
687	    """-> (markdown, sidecar). REFUSES to render an invalid report; it does not skip bad nodes.
688	
689	    Skipping is what a composer does. A composer that drops the sentences it cannot justify still
690	    produces a document, and the document it produces is the one that gets published. THIS returns
691	    nothing at all unless EVERY node is lawful — which is why `publisher.py` can trust what it is
692	    handed, and why it re-validates anyway.
693	    """
694	    fails = validate_report(nodes, b)
695	    if fails:
696	        raise ValueError(f'{len(fails)} unlawful node(s); NOTHING IS RENDERED:\n  - ' +
697	                         '\n  - '.join(str(f) for f in fails[:25]))
698	    md: list[str] = []
699	    sidecar: list[dict] = []
700	    para: list[str] = []
701	    form_seed = 0
702	
703	    def flush():
704	        if para:
705	            md.append(' '.join(para))
706	            md.append('')
707	            para.clear()
708	
709	    for n in nodes:
710	        if isinstance(n, ParagraphBreak):
711	            flush()
712	        elif isinstance(n, Heading):
713	            flush()
714	            md.append(f"{'#' * n.level} {n.text}")
715	            md.append('')
716	        elif isinstance(n, Attributed):
717	            s = _fmt_sentence(n, b, form_seed)
718	            form_seed += 1
719	            para.append(s)
720	            for cl in n.clauses:
721	                r = b.resolve(cl.card_id)
722	                sidecar.append(dict(
723	                    sentence_hash=sentence_hash(s), sentence=s, voice='ATTRIBUTED',
724	                    card_id=cl.card_id, manifestation_id=r.manifestation_id,
725	                    content_hash=r.content_hash, span_start=r.span_start, span_end=r.span_end,
726	                    span=r.span, work_id=r.work_id, expression_id=r.expression_id,
727	                    names_expression_id=r.names_expression_id, policy=b.policy.name,
728	                    attribution=r.attribution))
729	        elif isinstance(n, Owned):
730	            s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', n.text.strip())
731	            para.append(s)
732	            sidecar.append(dict(sentence_hash=sentence_hash(s), sentence=s, voice='OWNED',
733	                                premise_ids=list(n.premise_ids), card_id=None, manifestation_id=None,
734	                                content_hash=None, policy=b.policy.name))
735	        elif isinstance(n, EvidenceTable):
736	            flush()
737	            t = _table(n, b)
738	            if t:
739	                md.append(t)
740	                md.append('')
741	                for cid in n.card_ids:
742	                    r = b.resolve(cid)
743	                    row = f'TABLE_ROW::{cid}'
744	                    sidecar.append(dict(sentence_hash=sentence_hash(row), sentence=row, voice='TABLE',
745	                                        card_id=cid, manifestation_id=r.manifestation_id,
746	                                        content_hash=r.content_hash, span_start=r.span_start,
747	                                        span_end=r.span_end, span=r.span, work_id=r.work_id,
748	                                        expression_id=r.expression_id,
749	                                        names_expression_id=r.names_expression_id,
750	                                        policy=b.policy.name, attribution=r.attribution))
751	    flush()
752	    out = re.sub(r'\n{3,}', '\n\n', '\n'.join(md)).strip() + '\n'
753	    # "Writing in the {venue}" + venue "The Quarterly Journal of Economics" -> "in the The Quarterly".
754	    # `_venue_the()` already prevents it at the source; this is belt-and-braces ON THE RENDERED BYTES,
755	    # because the last time this correction existed it ran AFTER write_text() on a dead variable and the
756	    # disk kept the broken string while the metrics described the fixed one.
757	    out = re.sub(r'\bthe The\b', 'The', out)
758	    return out, sidecar
759

content.text: This is a rigorous chain. I now understand every refusal path. Two things I must resolve before building: how "landing page" / completeness is modeled (the `from_json` loader refuses a stored `complete` it can't re-derive), and whether assertion 6's "coverage derivations for every claimed gap" is actually enforced anywhere.
